text.title: UI Library Directory Structure and Conventions
text.summary: Three-layer directory layout, naming conventions, export rules, and bundle-size enforcement for a shared UI component library.

rule.statement: Organize into three layers: primitives/ (single responsibility), composite/ (composed from primitives), patterns/ (complex reusable UI patterns), and layout/ (page chrome).
rule.rationale: Clear layer separation prevents primitives from importing composites and keeps bundle tree-shaking effective.

example.code: src/ ├── components/ │ ├── primitives/ # Button, Input, Text, Box │ ├── composite/ # Card, Modal, Dropdown, Form │ ├── patterns/ # DataTable, Pagination, SearchInput │ └── layout/ # Container, Stack, Grid, Flex ├── hooks/ ├── utils/ ├── tokens/ └── index.ts # public barrel with sideEffects: false

rule.statement: Use named exports only. Set sideEffects: false in package.json. Never use default exports in library code.
rule.rationale: Default exports break refactoring tools and prevent tree-shaking; bundlers cannot drop unused named exports when sideEffects is missing.

rule.statement: Each component folder contains: ComponentName.tsx, ComponentName.test.tsx, ComponentName.stories.tsx, index.ts (re-export).
rule.rationale: Co-located test and story files ensure coverage and documentation stay in sync with the component.

section.title: Bundle Size Budget

rule.statement: Enforce per-export bundle size limits in CI using size-limit. Set limits before the first publish and tighten after each release.
rule.rationale: Bundle regressions from transitive deps (lodash, date-fns) are invisible without automated budgets.

section.reference: Bundle size budget config for CI enforcement

section.title: Story-First Development

rule.statement: Write the Storybook story before the component implementation. The story is the agent's understanding check and the designer's acceptance criterion.
rule.rationale: Story-first forces the API to be decided before implementation and prevents "no story" shortcuts under deadline.

section.reference: Scaffold script that creates story-first component skeleton
